learningObjectives: Name and use twelve body and health words at Movers A1 level.
Use have got to describe symptoms (I've got a headache).
Use should to give and understand simple health advice (You should rest).
Read a short text about feeling ill and match definitions to words.
Listen for health problems and advice in short dialogues.
Write a short note about feeling unwell (at least 15 words).
Talk about symptoms and health advice in short spoken answers.
grammarFocus: Have got: I've got a headache
Should for advice: You should rest

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: Minh has got a pain in his head.

vocabularyBank.exampleTranslation: Bụng tôi đau sau bữa trưa.

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: The doctor looks at Minh's tooth.
vocabularyBank.exampleTranslation: Bác sĩ kiểm tra răng của Minh.
Y tá kiểm tra cánh tay của Minh.

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: The doctor says Minh should rest.
vocabularyBank.exampleTranslation: Bác sĩ bảo Minh nên nghỉ ngơi.

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: Mum takes Minh to the hospital.
vocabularyBank.exampleTranslation: Mẹ đưa Minh đến bệnh viện.

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: A kind nurse helps Minh at the hospital.
vocabularyBank.exampleTranslation: Một y tá tốt bụng giúp Minh ở bệnh viện.

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: Minh is ill and cannot go to school.
vocabularyBank.exampleTranslation: Minh bị ốm và không thể đi học.
Hôm nay tôi bị đau đầu.

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: You should take this medicine twice a day.
vocabularyBank.exampleTranslation: Bạn nên uống thuốc này hai lần mỗi ngày.

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: You should rest when you feel sick.
vocabularyBank.exampleTranslation: Bạn nên nghỉ ngơi khi cảm thấy mệt/ốm.
Minh cảm thấy mệt/ốm ở trường.

grammarReference.structure: Have got: I've got a headache
grammarReference.explanation: Use have got (British English) to say you have a problem or symptom. I've got = I have got. For he/she/it use has got: He's got a headache. In questions: Have you got a headache?
examples: Minh has got a pain in his head.
commonMistakes: I have got headache (×) → I've got a headache (✓)
He have got a toothache (×) → He's got a toothache (✓)
I've got headache (×) → I've got a headache (✓) — need a/an

grammarReference.structure: Should for advice: You should rest
grammarReference.explanation: Use should + base verb to give friendly advice. You should rest. You shouldn't go to school when you are ill. Should comes before the main verb without to.
examples: You shouldn't play football when you are ill.
The doctor says you should drink water.
commonMistakes: You should to rest (×) → You should rest (✓)
You should rests (×) → You should rest (✓)
You shouldn't goes to school (×) → You shouldn't go to school (✓)

grammarReference.structure: Feel + adjective: feel sick / feel ill
grammarReference.explanation: Use feel + adjective to describe how your body is. Feel sick = cảm thấy mệt/ốm. Feel ill = cảm thấy bệnh. In questions: Do you feel sick?
examples: She feels better after she rests.
commonMistakes: I feel sickly today (×) → I feel sick today (✓)
He feels a sick (×) → He feels sick (✓)
Do you feel sickly? (×) → Do you feel sick? (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Body Parts
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and use head, stomach, tooth and arm in short health sentences.

exercises.title: Learn: Body Part Words
exercises.instructions: Read each clue. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: You think with this part of your body. It is your _____.
questions.explanation: Head (đầu) — nơi bạn suy nghĩ và thường bị đau đầu.

questions.questionText: Minh's _____ hurts after he eats bad food.
questions.explanation: Stomach (bụng/dạ dày) — đau bụng sau khi ăn.

questions.questionText: The doctor looks inside Minh's mouth at his _____.
questions.explanation: Tooth (răng) — bác sĩ kiểm tra răng trong miệng.

questions.questionText: The nurse checks Minh's _____ at the hospital.
questions.explanation: Arm (cánh tay) — y tá kiểm tra cánh tay.

choices.distractorNote: Là thuốc, không phải bộ phận

exercises.title: Practice: Match Body Parts
exercises.instructions: Match each word to what it means.

questions.questionText: Match the body part words.
questions.explanation: Mỗi từ chỉ một bộ phận cơ thể rõ ràng.

pairs.right: the top part of your body with your brain
the middle part of your body where food goes
hard white part in your mouth for biting
the part between your shoulder and your hand

exercises.title: Check: Body Parts Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: I've got a pain in my _____. (thinking part)
questions.explanation: Head — đau ở đầu.

questions.questionText: My _____ hurts. I shouldn't eat more food.
questions.explanation: Stomach — đau bụng, không nên ăn thêm.

questions.questionText: Open your mouth. The doctor checks your _____.
questions.explanation: Tooth — bác sĩ xem răng trong miệng.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete Body Sentences
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct body word.

questions.questionText: Complete: My [0] hurts. | The nurse checks my [1]. | The doctor looks at my [2].
questions.explanation: head; arm; tooth — ba bộ phận khác nhau.

content.template: My [0] hurts. The nurse checks my [1]. The doctor looks at my [2].

exercises.title: Review: Body Parts Mix
exercises.instructions: Mixed review from this lesson. Choose carefully.

questions.questionText: Minh has got a pain in his _____.
questions.explanation: Head — đau đầu trong bài đọc.

questions.questionText: Food goes to your _____.
questions.explanation: Stomach — thức ăn vào dạ dày.

questions.questionText: You use your _____ to hold things.
questions.explanation: Arm — cánh tay giúp cầm đồ (cùng với tay).

lessons.title: Lesson 2: People and Places
lessons.learningObjective: Use doctor, hospital and nurse to talk about getting help when ill.

exercises.title: Learn: Doctor, Nurse, Hospital
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: A _____ helps sick people and gives medicine.
questions.explanation: Doctor (bác sĩ) — chữa bệnh và kê thuốc.

questions.questionText: Mum takes Minh to the _____.
questions.explanation: Hospital (bệnh viện) — nơi khám bệnh.

questions.questionText: A _____ checks Minh's arm at the hospital.
questions.explanation: Nurse (y tá) — hỗ trợ bác sĩ, kiểm tra bệnh nhân.

questions.questionText: The _____ says: 'You should rest and take medicine.'
questions.explanation: Doctor — bác sĩ đưa lời khuyên y tế.

exercises.title: Practice: Match People and Places
exercises.instructions: Match each word to the correct description.

questions.questionText: Match health people and places.
questions.explanation: Doctor, nurse, hospital — vai trò và địa điểm khác nhau.

pairs.right: a person who treats sick people
a person who helps the doctor and patients
a place where sick people go for help
something you take when you are ill

exercises.title: Check: People and Places Quiz
exercises.instructions: Choose the best answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: Where does Minh go when he is very ill?
questions.explanation: Hospital — bệnh viện trong bài đọc.

questions.questionText: Who checks Minh's arm?
questions.explanation: Nurse — y tá kiểm tra cánh tay.

questions.questionText: The _____ looks at Minh's tooth.
questions.explanation: Doctor — bác sĩ xem răng.

exercises.title: Apply: Hospital Sentences
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences about the hospital.

questions.questionText: Complete: Mum takes Minh to the [0]. | A [1] checks his arm. | The [2] gives him medicine.
questions.explanation: hospital; nurse; doctor — thứ tự hợp lý.

content.template: Mum takes Minh to the [0]. A [1] checks his arm. The [2] gives him medicine.

exercises.title: Review: People and Places Mix
exercises.instructions: Mixed review. No hints.

questions.questionText: A person who works at a hospital and helps the doctor is a _____.
questions.explanation: Nurse (y tá) — hỗ trợ bác sĩ.

questions.questionText: Minh is ill, so he goes to the _____.
questions.explanation: Hospital — đi bệnh viện khi ốm nặng.

questions.questionText: The _____ tells Minh to take medicine.
questions.explanation: Doctor — bác sĩ kê thuốc.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Feeling Unwell
lessons.learningObjective: Use ill, headache, medicine, rest and feel sick to describe health problems.

exercises.title: Learn: Illness Words
exercises.instructions: Choose the word that best completes each sentence.

questions.questionText: Minh is _____ and cannot go to school.
questions.explanation: Ill (ốm) — không đi học được.

questions.questionText: I've got a _____. My head hurts.
questions.explanation: Headache (đau đầu) — đau ở đầu.

questions.questionText: You should take this _____ twice a day.
questions.explanation: Medicine (thuốc) — uống thuốc theo chỉ dẫn.

questions.questionText: When you are ill, you should _____ at home.
questions.explanation: Rest (nghỉ ngơi) — nghỉ ở nhà khi ốm.

exercises.title: Practice: Match Illness Words
exercises.instructions: Match each word or phrase to its meaning.

questions.questionText: Match the illness vocabulary.
questions.explanation: Ill, headache, feel sick, rest, medicine — từ về ốm và chữa trị.

pairs.right: feel unwell or want to vomit
stop activity and relax to get better

exercises.title: Check: Illness Challenge
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: Minh _____ at school. His teacher calls his mum.
questions.explanation: Feels sick — cảm thấy mệt/ốm ở trường.

questions.questionText: The doctor says Minh is _____.
questions.explanation: Ill — bác sĩ nói Minh bị ốm.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: I've got a headache — cấu trúc have got + triệu chứng.

exercises.title: Apply: How Do You Feel?
exercises.instructions: Fill in with ill, headache, medicine or rest.

questions.questionText: Complete: I feel sick. I've got a [0]. | The doctor gives me [1]. | I should [2] at home because I am [3].
questions.explanation: headache; medicine; rest; ill — câu chuyện ốm điển hình.

content.template: I feel sick. I've got a [0]. The doctor gives me [1]. I should [2] at home because I am [3].

exercises.title: Review: Illness Words Mix
exercises.instructions: Choose the best illness word.

questions.questionText: When your head hurts, you've got a _____.
questions.explanation: Headache — đau đầu.

questions.questionText: You are _____ so you shouldn't go to school.
questions.explanation: Ill — ốm thì nên nghỉ học.

questions.questionText: The doctor says: You should _____ and take medicine.
questions.explanation: Rest — nghỉ ngơi kết hợp uống thuốc.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Have Got
lessons.learningObjective: Use have got / has got to describe symptoms (I've got a headache).

exercises.title: Learn: Have Got
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct form.

questions.questionText: Minh _____ a headache.
questions.explanation: Minh (he) → has got.

questions.questionText: I _____ a stomach ache today.
questions.explanation: I → have got.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: I've got a headache — rút gọn và có mạo từ a.

questions.questionText: _____ you got a toothache?
questions.explanation: Have you got — câu hỏi yes/no.

choices.distractorNote: Do you have cũng đúng nhưng câu này dùng Have got

exercises.title: Practice: Complete Have Got
exercises.instructions: Fill in have got or has got.

questions.questionText: Complete: I [0] a headache. | Minh [1] a bad tooth. | We [2] stomach aches today.
questions.explanation: have got; has got; have got — I/Minh/we.

content.template: I [0] a headache. Minh [1] a bad tooth. We [2] stomach aches today.

exercises.title: Check: Have Got Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer.

questions.questionText: Minh tells the doctor: I _____ a headache.
questions.explanation: Minh nói về bản thân → I've got.

questions.questionText: Which question is correct?
questions.explanation: Have you got a headache? — câu hỏi chuẩn.

questions.questionText: She _____ a pain in her head.
questions.explanation: She → has got.

exercises.title: Apply: Build Have Got Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order.

questions.questionText: Make: I've / got / a / headache / today.
questions.explanation: I've got a headache today.

questions.questionText: Make: Minh / has / got / a / bad / tooth.
questions.explanation: Minh has got a bad tooth.

exercises.title: Review: Have Got Mix
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct form.

questions.questionText: They _____ stomach aches after lunch.
questions.explanation: They → have got.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: He's got a toothache — he has got rút gọn.

questions.questionText: _____ she got a headache?
questions.explanation: Has she got — câu hỏi với she.

choices.distractorNote: Does she have cũng được nhưng không khớp got

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Should for Advice
lessons.learningObjective: Use should and shouldn't to give simple health advice.

exercises.title: Learn: Should + Verb
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct advice sentence.

questions.questionText: You are ill. You _____ rest at home.
questions.explanation: Should + động từ nguyên mẫu rest.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: You should take medicine — should + take.

choices.text: You should to take this medicine.

questions.questionText: When you are ill, you _____ go to school.
questions.explanation: Shouldn't — lời khuyên phủ định.

questions.questionText: The doctor says: You _____ drink water.
questions.explanation: Should drink — lời khuyên tích cực.

exercises.title: Practice: Should or Shouldn't
exercises.instructions: Complete with should or shouldn't.

questions.questionText: Complete: You [0] rest when you are ill. | You [1] play football today. | You [2] take your medicine.
questions.explanation: should; shouldn't; should — nghỉ/uống thuốc nên; chơi bóng không nên.

content.template: You [0] rest when you are ill. You [1] play football today. You [2] take your medicine.

exercises.title: Check: Advice Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the best advice.

questions.questionText: Minh has got a headache. Best advice:
questions.explanation: You should rest — nghỉ khi đau đầu.

choices.text: You should to rest at home.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is NOT good advice?
questions.explanation: You shouldn't eat bad food — đúng; câu sai là should to go.

choices.text: You should to go to school when you are ill.

questions.questionText: Mum says: He _____ see a doctor.
questions.explanation: Should see — he should + see.

exercises.title: Apply: Order Advice Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order.

questions.questionText: Make: You / should / rest / and / take / medicine.
questions.explanation: You should rest and take medicine.

questions.questionText: Make: You / shouldn't / go / to / school / today.
questions.explanation: You shouldn't go to school today.

exercises.title: Review: Should Mix
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct advice sentence.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: You should drink water — should + drink.

choices.text: You should drink water when you are ill.

questions.questionText: Linh is ill. She _____ play outside.
questions.explanation: Shouldn't — không nên chơi ngoài trời.

questions.questionText: The doctor says: You _____ take this medicine twice a day.
questions.explanation: Should take — lời dặn của bác sĩ.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Feel + Adjective & Health Questions
lessons.learningObjective: Use feel sick/ill and ask health questions with Do you feel…? / Have you got…?

exercises.title: Learn: Feel Sick / Feel Ill
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct sentence.

questions.questionText: Minh _____ sick at school.
questions.explanation: Minh (he) → feels sick.

questions.questionText: I don't _____ well today.
questions.explanation: Feel well — feel + adjective.

questions.questionText: _____ you feel ill?
questions.explanation: Do you feel — câu hỏi present simple.

choices.distractorNote: Are you ill — khác cấu trúc

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: She feels ill — feel + ill, không thêm a.

exercises.title: Practice: Complete Health Sentences
exercises.instructions: Fill in the gaps.

questions.questionText: Complete: Minh [0] sick at school. | [1] you feel ill? | Yes, I [2] got a headache.
questions.explanation: feels; Do; have/'ve — cảm giác, câu hỏi, triệu chứng.

content.template: Minh [0] sick at school. [1] you feel ill? Yes, I [2] got a headache.

exercises.title: Check: Health Questions
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct question or answer.

questions.questionText: Which question is correct?
questions.explanation: Do you feel sick? — câu hỏi chuẩn.

questions.questionText: Best answer: Have you got a headache?
questions.explanation: Yes, I've got a headache — trả lời ngắn tự nhiên.

choices.text: Yes, I have got a headache wrong.

questions.questionText: The teacher says: Minh _____ sick.
questions.explanation: Feels sick — he feels.

exercises.title: Apply: Build Health Questions
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order.

questions.questionText: Make: Do / you / feel / sick / today?
questions.explanation: Do you feel sick today?

questions.questionText: Make: Have / you / got / a / stomach / ache?
questions.explanation: Have you got a stomach ache?

exercises.title: Review: Feel and Questions Mix
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct sentence.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: I feel sick — I + feel.

choices.text: I feel sick and I've got a headache.
I feel sickly and have got headache.

questions.questionText: _____ Minh feel ill today?
questions.explanation: Does Minh feel — third person question.

questions.questionText: Best advice after 'I feel ill':
questions.explanation: You should rest — kết hợp feel ill + should.

choices.text: You should rest and see a doctor.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Read for Gist
lessons.learningObjective: Read a short text about feeling ill and identify the main idea.

exercises.title: Learn: Minh Feels Ill
exercises.instructions: Read the passage. Answer the questions.

passage.title: Minh Feels Ill
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi. This morning Minh does not feel well. He has got a headache and his stomach hurts.

At school Minh tells his teacher. The teacher calls Minh's mum. Mum takes Minh to the hospital. A kind nurse checks his arm and takes his temperature.

The doctor looks at Minh's tooth and says, "You are ill. You should rest today. Take this medicine twice a day." Minh goes home and rests on the sofa. His friend Linh sends a message: "Get well soon!"

questions.questionText: Who is the text about?
questions.explanation: The text is about Minh, a nine-year-old boy in Hanoi.

questions.questionText: What is the text mainly about?
questions.explanation: Toàn bài kể Minh ốm và đi bệnh viện.

choices.text: Minh feeling ill and going to hospital

exercises.title: Practice: School or Hospital?
exercises.instructions: Read again. Decide where each event happens.

passage.title: Minh Feels Ill
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi. This morning Minh does not feel well. He has got a headache and his stomach hurts.

At school Minh tells his teacher. The teacher calls Minh's mum. Mum takes Minh to the hospital. A kind nurse checks his arm and takes his temperature.

The doctor looks at Minh's tooth and says, "You are ill. You should rest today. Take this medicine twice a day." Minh goes home and rests on the sofa. His friend Linh sends a message: "Get well soon!"

questions.questionText: Where does Minh first feel sick?
questions.explanation: At school Minh tells his teacher — ốm ở trường trước.

questions.questionText: Where does the doctor look at Minh's tooth?
questions.explanation: At the hospital — bác sĩ khám ở bệnh viện.

exercises.title: Check: Main Idea
exercises.instructions: Read carefully. These questions need thinking.

passage.title: Minh Feels Ill
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi. This morning Minh does not feel well. He has got a headache and his stomach hurts.

At school Minh tells his teacher. The teacher calls Minh's mum. Mum takes Minh to the hospital. A kind nurse checks his arm and takes his temperature.

The doctor looks at Minh's tooth and says, "You are ill. You should rest today. Take this medicine twice a day." Minh goes home and rests on the sofa. His friend Linh sends a message: "Get well soon!"

questions.questionText: What is the best title for this text?
questions.explanation: Chủ đề chính: Minh ốm → Minh Feels Ill.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is NOT true about Minh?
questions.explanation: Minh goes to hospital — không ở nhà suốt.

choices.text: Minh stays at home all day and never goes to hospital.
The doctor tells him to rest.

exercises.title: Apply: Match Story Events
exercises.instructions: Use the passage. Match each person to what they do.

At school Minh tells his teacher. The teacher calls Minh's mum. Mum takes Minh to the hospital. A kind nurse checks his arm and takes his temperature.

The doctor looks at Minh's tooth and says, "You are ill. You should rest today. Take this medicine twice a day." Minh goes home and rests on the sofa. His friend Linh sends a message: "Get well soon!"

questions.explanation: Teacher calls mum; nurse checks arm; doctor gives advice.

exercises.title: Review: Story Overview
exercises.instructions: Read the passage one more time. Mixed review.

passage.title: Minh Feels Ill
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi. This morning Minh does not feel well. He has got a headache and his stomach hurts.

At school Minh tells his teacher. The teacher calls Minh's mum. Mum takes Minh to the hospital. A kind nurse checks his arm and takes his temperature.

The doctor looks at Minh's tooth and says, "You are ill. You should rest today. Take this medicine twice a day." Minh goes home and rests on the sofa. His friend Linh sends a message: "Get well soon!"

questions.questionText: Where does Minh live?
questions.explanation: He lives in Hanoi — đoạn đầu bài đọc.

questions.questionText: What should Minh do at home?
questions.explanation: Rest on the sofa — nghỉ ngơi ở nhà.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Find Details
lessons.learningObjective: Find specific symptoms, people and advice in a health text.

exercises.title: Learn: Find the Symptoms
exercises.instructions: Read the passage. Find the details.

passage.title: Minh Feels Ill
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi. This morning Minh does not feel well. He has got a headache and his stomach hurts.

At school Minh tells his teacher. The teacher calls Minh's mum. Mum takes Minh to the hospital. A kind nurse checks his arm and takes his temperature.

The doctor looks at Minh's tooth and says, "You are ill. You should rest today. Take this medicine twice a day." Minh goes home and rests on the sofa. His friend Linh sends a message: "Get well soon!"

questions.questionText: What has Minh got at the start?
questions.explanation: He has got a headache and his stomach hurts.

questions.questionText: Who takes Minh to the hospital?
questions.explanation: Mum takes Minh to the hospital.

exercises.title: Practice: True or False?
exercises.instructions: Read again. Choose the correct statement.

passage.title: Minh Feels Ill
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi. This morning Minh does not feel well. He has got a headache and his stomach hurts.

At school Minh tells his teacher. The teacher calls Minh's mum. Mum takes Minh to the hospital. A kind nurse checks his arm and takes his temperature.

The doctor looks at Minh's tooth and says, "You are ill. You should rest today. Take this medicine twice a day." Minh goes home and rests on the sofa. His friend Linh sends a message: "Get well soon!"

questions.explanation: A kind nurse checks his arm — đúng.

questions.questionText: Minh should take medicine once a year.
questions.explanation: Twice a day — không phải once a year.

exercises.title: Check: Detail Search
exercises.instructions: Find the answers in the text.

passage.title: Minh Feels Ill
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi. This morning Minh does not feel well. He has got a headache and his stomach hurts.

At school Minh tells his teacher. The teacher calls Minh's mum. Mum takes Minh to the hospital. A kind nurse checks his arm and takes his temperature.

The doctor looks at Minh's tooth and says, "You are ill. You should rest today. Take this medicine twice a day." Minh goes home and rests on the sofa. His friend Linh sends a message: "Get well soon!"

questions.questionText: What does the doctor look at?
questions.explanation: The doctor looks at Minh's tooth.

questions.questionText: How often should Minh take the medicine?
questions.explanation: Take this medicine twice a day.

questions.questionText: What message does Linh send?
questions.explanation: Get well soon! — chúc mau khỏe.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete from the Text
exercises.instructions: Fill in the gaps using words from the passage.

passage.title: Minh Feels Ill
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi. This morning Minh does not feel well. He has got a headache and his stomach hurts.

At school Minh tells his teacher. The teacher calls Minh's mum. Mum takes Minh to the hospital. A kind nurse checks his arm and takes his temperature.

The doctor looks at Minh's tooth and says, "You are ill. You should rest today. Take this medicine twice a day." Minh goes home and rests on the sofa. His friend Linh sends a message: "Get well soon!"

questions.questionText: Complete: Minh has got a [0]. | Mum takes him to the [1]. | The doctor says he should [2].
questions.explanation: headache; hospital; rest — chi tiết từ bài đọc.

content.template: Minh has got a [0]. Mum takes him to the [1]. The doctor says he should [2].

exercises.title: Review: Detail Search Mix
exercises.instructions: Find the answers in the text again.

passage.title: Minh Feels Ill
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi. This morning Minh does not feel well. He has got a headache and his stomach hurts.

At school Minh tells his teacher. The teacher calls Minh's mum. Mum takes Minh to the hospital. A kind nurse checks his arm and takes his temperature.

The doctor looks at Minh's tooth and says, "You are ill. You should rest today. Take this medicine twice a day." Minh goes home and rests on the sofa. His friend Linh sends a message: "Get well soon!"

questions.explanation: Rests on the sofa at home.

choices.text: In the hospital bed all night

The doctor looks at Minh's tooth and says, "You are ill. You should rest today. Take this medicine twice a day." Minh goes home and rests on the sofa. His friend Linh sends a message: "Get well soon!"

lessons.title: Lesson 1: At the Doctor
lessons.learningObjective: Listen to a doctor dialogue and understand symptoms and advice.

exercises.title: Learn: At the Doctor
exercises.instructions: Listen to Minh and the doctor. Answer the questions.

script.title: At the Doctor
script.setting: A small hospital room in Hanoi, quiet afternoon

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: Hello Minh. What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: I've got a headache, Doctor.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: Have you got a stomach ache too?

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Yes, my stomach hurts.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: Open your mouth, please. You've got a bad tooth.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: You should rest today. Take this medicine twice a day.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Thank you, Doctor.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: You can go home now, Minh. Rest well!

script.audioNotes: Calm doctor–patient dialogue with a nurse closing line. Moderate pace. Approx. 50 seconds.

questions.questionText: What has Minh got?
questions.explanation: I've got a headache, Doctor.

questions.questionText: Does Minh have a stomach ache?
questions.explanation: Yes, my stomach hurts.

exercises.title: Practice: More Doctor Details
exercises.instructions: Listen again. Answer the next questions.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Calling Mum from School
lessons.learningObjective: Listen to a school phone call and understand a simple health context.

exercises.title: Learn: Phone Call from School
exercises.instructions: Listen to the teacher, Mum and Minh. Answer the questions.

script.title: Calling Mum from School
script.setting: School office, phone call to Minh's home

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Hello, Mrs Tran. This is Minh's teacher.

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: Hello. Is Minh OK?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Minh feels sick. He's got a headache.

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: Oh no! Should I come to school?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Yes, please. He's resting in the office.

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: I'll come now. He should see a doctor.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Mum, my arm doesn't hurt. It's my head.

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: Don't worry. We'll go to the hospital.

script.audioNotes: Phone-style dialogue: teacher, mum, then Minh speaks. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.questionText: Who calls Minh's mum?
questions.explanation: This is Minh's teacher — giáo viên gọi.

questions.questionText: How does Minh feel?
questions.explanation: Minh feels sick. He's got a headache.

exercises.title: Practice: Listen for Advice
exercises.instructions: Listen again. Focus on should and advice.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Write About Feeling Ill
lessons.learningObjective: Use frames to write short sentences about symptoms with have got.

exercises.title: Learn: Symptom Writing Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences about feeling ill.

questions.questionText: Complete: I feel [0]. | I've [1] a headache. | I am [2] and I cannot go to school.
questions.explanation: sick; got; ill — khung viết khi ốm.

content.template: I feel [0]. I've [1] a headache. I am [2] and I cannot go to school.

exercises.title: Practice: Order Symptom Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order.

questions.questionText: Make: I / feel / sick / today.
questions.explanation: I feel sick today.

exercises.title: Check: Write About Feeling Ill
exercises.instructions: Write about how you feel when you are ill. Use at least 15 words.

content.taskDescription: Write a short note to your teacher explaining that you feel sick today. Say what hurts and use have got at least once.
prompts: What have you got? (headache, stomach ache, etc.)
successCriteria: Uses have got for a symptom

modelAnswer.text: Dear Teacher, I feel sick today. I've got a headache and my stomach hurts. I am ill and I cannot come to school. I should rest at home. Thank you.

grammar.criteria: Uses have got and should correctly for health contexts.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses body and health words from the unit (headache, medicine, rest, etc.).

organization.criteria: Ideas follow a logical order or answer all prompts clearly.

taskAchievement.criteria: Meets minimum length and addresses the writing task.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi.

exercises.title: Apply: Choose the Best Sentence
exercises.instructions: Choose the best written sentence for each situation.

questions.questionText: Best sentence about a headache:
questions.explanation: I've got a headache — have got + triệu chứng.

choices.text: I've got a headache and my head hurts.

questions.questionText: Best note to a teacher:
questions.explanation: I feel sick — lịch sự và rõ ràng.

choices.text: Dear Teacher, I feel sick today. I cannot come to school.

exercises.title: Review: Symptom Writing Quiz
exercises.instructions: Choose the best way to complete each writing frame.

questions.questionText: Complete: Today I [0] sick. My stomach [1] and I've got a [2].
questions.explanation: feel; hurts; headache — ôn khung viết triệu chứng.

content.template: Today I [0] sick. My stomach [1] and I've got a [2].

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Write Health Advice
lessons.learningObjective: Write 2–3 connected sentences giving advice with should.

exercises.title: Learn: Advice Sentences
exercises.instructions: Complete sentences with should.

questions.questionText: Complete: You [0] rest at home. | You [1] take your medicine. | You [2] go to school when you are ill.
questions.explanation: should; should; shouldn't — lời khuyên.

content.template: You [0] rest at home. You [1] take your medicine. You [2] go to school when you are ill.

exercises.title: Practice: Order Advice Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put words in order to make advice.

questions.questionText: Make: You / should / rest / and / drink / water.
questions.explanation: You should rest and drink water.

exercises.title: Check: Write Health Advice
exercises.instructions: Write advice for a friend who is ill. Use at least 15 words.

content.taskDescription: Your friend Linh has got a headache. Write a short message with two pieces of advice using should.
prompts: What medicine or rest advice can you give?

modelAnswer.text: Hi Linh, you should rest at home today. You shouldn't go to school. You should drink water and take your medicine. I hope you feel better soon!

grammar.criteria: Uses have got and should correctly for health contexts.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses body and health words from the unit (headache, medicine, rest, etc.).

organization.criteria: Ideas follow a logical order or answer all prompts clearly.

taskAchievement.criteria: Meets minimum length and addresses the writing task.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi.

exercises.title: Apply: Best Advice Message
exercises.instructions: Choose the best short message.

questions.questionText: Best message to ill friend Linh:
questions.explanation: Should rest + shouldn't go to school — lời khuyên rõ.

choices.text: Hi Linh, you should rest. You shouldn't go to school today.

exercises.title: Review: Advice Writing Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the advice writing frames.

questions.questionText: Complete: When you have a headache, you [0] rest. You [1] play outside. You [2] drink water.
questions.explanation: should; shouldn't; should — ôn lời khuyên.

content.template: When you have a headache, you [0] rest. You [1] play outside. You [2] drink water.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: A Doctor Visit
lessons.learningObjective: Write a short connected message about a hospital visit.

exercises.title: Learn: Hospital Visit Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete sentences about the doctor and hospital.

questions.questionText: Complete: Mum took me to the [0]. | The [1] checked my arm. | The doctor gave me [2].
questions.explanation: hospital; nurse; medicine — chuyến khám.

content.template: Mum took me to the [0]. The [1] checked my arm. The doctor gave me [2].

exercises.title: Practice: Order Hospital Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the sentence parts in logical order.

questions.questionText: Make: The doctor / said / I / should / rest / and / take / medicine.
questions.explanation: The doctor said I should rest and take medicine.

exercises.title: Check: Write About a Doctor Visit
exercises.instructions: Write about going to the doctor or hospital. Use at least 15 words.

content.taskDescription: Write a short message to your pen friend about when Minh went to the hospital. Say what the doctor and nurse did and what Minh should do.
prompts: What should Minh do at home?

modelAnswer.text: Minh went to the hospital with his mum. The nurse checked his arm. The doctor said he is ill and he has got a bad tooth. He should rest and take medicine twice a day.

grammar.criteria: Uses have got and should correctly for health contexts.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses body and health words from the unit (headache, medicine, rest, etc.).

organization.criteria: Ideas follow a logical order or answer all prompts clearly.

taskAchievement.criteria: Meets minimum length and addresses the writing task.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi.

exercises.title: Apply: Choose the Best Paragraph
exercises.instructions: Choose the best short message about a doctor visit.

questions.questionText: Best message to a pen friend:
questions.explanation: Hospital + doctor + should rest — đủ thông tin.

choices.text: I went to the hospital. The doctor said I am ill. I should rest and take medicine.
I am medicine hospital rest should.

questions.questionText: Best sentence with have got:
questions.explanation: I've got a headache — đúng cấu trúc.

choices.text: I've got a headache so I went to the doctor.

exercises.title: Review: Doctor Visit Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete a short hospital message frame.

questions.questionText: Complete: At the hospital the [0] looked at my tooth. I [1] got a headache too. I should [2] at home.
questions.explanation: doctor; have/'ve; rest — ôn viết về bác sĩ.

content.template: At the hospital the [0] looked at my tooth. I [1] got a headache too. I should [2] at home.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Talk About Symptoms
lessons.learningObjective: Choose appropriate spoken phrases about feeling ill.

exercises.title: Learn: What to Say When Ill
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say.

questions.questionText: Someone asks: How do you feel? You say:
questions.explanation: I feel sick — câu trả lời tự nhiên.

questions.questionText: Best answer: Have you got a headache?
questions.explanation: Yes, I've got a headache — trả lời đầy đủ.

exercises.title: Practice: Symptom Replies
exercises.instructions: Choose the best reply.

questions.questionText: Your stomach hurts. Best thing to say:
questions.explanation: My stomach hurts — nói rõ triệu chứng.

questions.questionText: Tell the teacher you are ill. Best sentence:
questions.explanation: I feel sick — thông báo lịch sự.

choices.text: Teacher, I feel sick. I have got a headache.

exercises.title: Check: Talk About Symptoms
exercises.instructions: Answer the questions about feeling ill. Speak for up to 90 seconds.

content.prompt: The examiner asks about how you feel when you are ill.
content.sceneDescription: Story outline: (1) Minh at school holding his head, (2) teacher calling Mum on the phone, (3) Mum driving Minh to hospital, (4) doctor checking Minh's tooth.
followUpQuestions: How do you feel when you are ill?
Have you got a headache before?
What hurts when you feel sick?
Do you tell your teacher or your mum?
Where do you go when you are very ill?
suggestedAnswers: Yes, I've got a headache sometimes.
I go to the hospital with my mum.

assessmentCriteria.pronunciation: Health words (headache, stomach, medicine) are clear enough to understand.
assessmentCriteria.fluency: Answers are short but connected; brief pauses are acceptable.
assessmentCriteria.grammar: Uses have got and should in short phrases.
assessmentCriteria.vocabulary: Uses body and health vocabulary from the unit.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Quick Symptom Replies
exercises.instructions: Choose the best short spoken response.

questions.questionText: Doctor asks: What's wrong? You say:
questions.explanation: I've got a headache — trả lời bác sĩ.

exercises.title: Review: Symptom Speaking Quiz
exercises.instructions: Choose the best spoken answer.

questions.questionText: Describe your pain. Best phrase:
questions.explanation: My head hurts — mô tả đau đầu.

choices.text: My head hurts. I've got a headache.

questions.questionText: Say you cannot go to school. Best answer:
questions.explanation: I am ill — không đi học vì ốm.

choices.text: I am ill so I cannot go to school.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Give Health Advice
lessons.learningObjective: Use should and shouldn't in short spoken advice.

exercises.title: Learn: Say Should / Shouldn't
exercises.instructions: Choose the best spoken advice.

questions.questionText: Your friend is ill. You say:
questions.explanation: You should rest — lời khuyên chuẩn.

questions.questionText: Friend has a headache. You say:
questions.explanation: You shouldn't play football — không nên chơi.

exercises.title: Practice: Medicine Advice
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct spoken response.

questions.questionText: Doctor's advice — best thing to repeat:
questions.explanation: You should take this medicine — nhắc lại lời bác sĩ.

choices.text: You should take this medicine twice a day.

questions.questionText: Mum says Minh should see a doctor. Best reply:
questions.explanation: OK, we'll go to the hospital — đồng ý hợp lý.

choices.text: OK, Mum. We'll go to the hospital.

exercises.title: Check: Give Health Advice
exercises.instructions: Give advice using should. Speak for up to 90 seconds.

content.prompt: The examiner asks you to give advice to a friend who is ill.
content.sceneDescription: Linh is at home in bed with a headache. Minh wants to help. Labels: rest, medicine, water, don't go to school.
followUpQuestions: What should Linh do when she has a headache?
What do you say to a sick friend?
suggestedAnswers: No, she shouldn't go to school.
She should take medicine and drink water.

assessmentCriteria.pronunciation: Health words (headache, stomach, medicine) are clear enough to understand.
assessmentCriteria.fluency: Answers are short but connected; brief pauses are acceptable.
assessmentCriteria.grammar: Uses have got and should in short phrases.
assessmentCriteria.vocabulary: Uses body and health vocabulary from the unit.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Choose the Best Advice
exercises.instructions: Pick the most natural spoken advice.

questions.questionText: Linh has a headache. Best advice:
questions.explanation: Rest + drink water — lời khuyên đơn giản.

choices.text: You should rest and drink water.

exercises.title: Review: Advice Speaking Quiz
exercises.instructions: Choose the best advice using should.

questions.questionText: When you are ill, best advice to yourself:
questions.explanation: I should rest — tự nhắc bản thân.

choices.text: I should rest and take my medicine.

questions.questionText: What shouldn't you do when ill?
questions.explanation: You shouldn't go to school — nghỉ học.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Talk About the Doctor
lessons.learningObjective: Describe a doctor or hospital visit in short spoken answers.

exercises.title: Learn: Hospital Phrases
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say about the doctor.

questions.questionText: Where did you go when you were ill? Best answer:
questions.explanation: I went to the hospital — trả lời địa điểm.

choices.text: I went to the hospital with my mum.

questions.questionText: What did the doctor say? Best answer:
questions.explanation: You should rest — lời bác sĩ.

choices.text: The doctor said I should rest and take medicine.

exercises.title: Practice: Nurse and Doctor
exercises.instructions: Choose the best spoken answers about the hospital.

questions.questionText: Who checked your arm? Best answer:
questions.explanation: The nurse checked my arm — y tá.

questions.questionText: What did the doctor look at? Best answer:
questions.explanation: My tooth — bác sĩ xem răng.

choices.text: The doctor looked at my tooth.

exercises.title: Check: Talk About the Doctor
exercises.instructions: Describe a visit to the doctor or hospital. Speak for up to 90 seconds.

content.prompt: Tell the examiner about Minh's visit to the hospital.
content.sceneDescription: Timeline: feel sick at school → Mum arrives → hospital → nurse checks arm → doctor looks at tooth → go home and rest.
followUpQuestions: Why did Minh go to the hospital?
Who helped Minh at the hospital?
What should Minh do at home?
What did Linh say to Minh?
suggestedAnswers: Because he was ill and had a headache.
A nurse and a doctor helped him.
The doctor said he should rest and take medicine.
He should rest on the sofa.

assessmentCriteria.pronunciation: Health words (headache, stomach, medicine) are clear enough to understand.
assessmentCriteria.fluency: Answers are short but connected; brief pauses are acceptable.
assessmentCriteria.grammar: Uses have got and should in short phrases.
assessmentCriteria.vocabulary: Uses body and health vocabulary from the unit.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Tell the Story
exercises.instructions: Choose the best short spoken description.

questions.questionText: Best short story about Minh's day:
questions.explanation: Feel sick → hospital → rest — thứ tự rõ.

choices.text: Minh felt sick at school. Mum took him to hospital. The doctor said he should rest.
Minh hospital sick doctor rest should.

exercises.title: Review: Doctor Speaking Quiz
exercises.instructions: Choose the best spoken answer.

questions.questionText: What do you say to a sick friend? Best answer:
questions.explanation: Get well soon! — chúc mau khỏe.

questions.questionText: Describe the doctor's advice. Best pair:
questions.explanation: Rest + medicine — như trong bài.

choices.text: You should rest. You should take medicine twice a day.
